title: Song: The Robin.
content:
At misty dawn,
     At rosy morn,
    The Redbreast sings alone:
     At twilight dim,
     Still, still, his hymn
    Hath a sad, and sorrowing tone.

    Another day, his song is gay,
     For a listening bird is near 
    O ye who sorrow, come borrow, borrow,
     A lesson of robin here!
meaning:
Samuel Griswold Goodrich's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Song: The Robin."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
